arguments.task: Open and read official Coda first-party pages that support: product/API description, API authentication methods, API reference surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing or access tier for API use, and whether Coda has an official MCP server. Prefer canonical developer/docs/pricing/help pages. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted facts tied to each URL. Do not rely on search result snippets; open the pages.

input.thought: Start a focused browser run on official Coda sources and extract facts with URLs.

raw_result.description: Coda provides a REST API for reading and modifying docs, tables, rows, formulas, and other workspace data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Coda developer docs document OAuth 2.0 for apps and bearer-token authentication via API tokens/personal tokens in the Authentication section/reference examples.
raw_result.gating_reason: Coda exposes public developer documentation and pricing states Free and Paid Workspaces; no contact-sales or partner approval is indicated for basic API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documentation covers docs, pages, tables, rows, columns, formulas, controls, analytics, webhooks, mutations, and account/workspace-related resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official developer API page references a Coda MCP server, indicating an existing official MCP offering.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The API reference includes rate limiting guidance; the browser extraction captured endpoint-specific limits including analytics reads at 100 requests per 6 seconds.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer hub, dedicated API reference, explicit authentication section, published pricing page, and an official MCP server reference are all positive signals for toolkit buildability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Concluded from partial browser evidence already gathered without opening the dedicated Authentication or MCP pages directly in this pass. Structured fields most likely correct, but auth_methods and mcp_exists rely on references/excerpts from the API page rather than separate fully read canonical auth/MCP pages.
